segments.s540-g33: نوشته here functions as the main verb/past participle 'wrote' (the subject is omitted, as is common in Persian). The following prepositional phrase بر آن حقه ('on that coffer') is an adverbial complement specifying location, and تاریخ آن ('its history') is the direct object; the usual object marker را is omitted for poetic concision and the word order is inverted compared with colloquial prose.
segments.s540-g34: پدیدار کرده is a compound meaning 'has made manifest' or 'made visible'—پدیدار is the thing made manifest and کرده is the verbal element 'made/done' used causatively/perfectively. بن و بیخ آن ('its root and origin') are coordinated objects (بن and بیخ are near‑synonyms meaning root/foundation and essence/origin) with آن referring back to the same item; again the subject is omitted and the verb precedes its objects in this poetic ordering.
segments.s540-g1: 'به دل گفت موبد' is an idiom meaning 'the mobed said to himself' — به + noun indicates direction into the mind ('to/in the heart'), گفت is the past tense 'said', and موبد is the subject. The following که begins the reported thought.
segments.s540-g2: که introduces the content clause ('that …'). فرمان چنین آمد is literal 'such an order came' (آمد here = 'came/was issued' in the past), and از شهریار 'from the king' gives the agent/source.
segments.s540-g3: همه 'all/everyone' is the topic; مرگ راییم is a compact, slightly archaic expression meaning 'our counsel/opinion is death' or 'we all resolve for death' (راییم functions as 'our decision/is our opinion' in older usage). برنا و پیر 'young and old' is a coordinating phrase emphasizing everyone.
segments.s540-g4: ندارد is the present negative 'does not have' (from داشتن). پسر شهریار اردشیر follows as the direct object/possessor phrase: 'the king Ardashir has no son.' Word order is verb + object phrase (typical Persian), with the proper name last.
segments.s540-g5: گر is the conditional 'if'. او is 'he'. بی‌عدد سالیان is a poetic/genitive phrase 'countless years'. بشمرد is a classical/hypothetical verbal form (subjunctive/optative sense) 'were to count' or 'should count', marking the unreal/conditional clause.
segments.s540-g6: به دشمن رسد تخت is a result clause: رسد (here = 'reach/attain/fall to') takes تخت 'the throne' as its subject and به دشمن 'to the enemy' as the beneficiary; چون بگذرد 'when/as it passes' uses چون + present/subjunctive بگذرد to mean 'when it passes' or 'once it passes'.
segments.s540-g7: همان به ... کزین کار ناسودمند opens with a pointing/emphatic 'that (very thing)': همان 'that/precisely', به کزین (کزین = کز + این, 'from this' / 'because of this'), کار 'affair', ناسودمند an adjective 'unprofitable/fruitless' giving the reason or characterization of the affair.
segments.s540-g8: به مردی یکی کار سازم بلند: به مردی 'to/for a man' marks the beneficiary/agent, یکی کار 'one (single) task/deed' is the object, and سازم is first-person present/subjunctive/intent 'I will make/perform/arrange' (classical Persian uses this form for 'I will do'). بلند is an adjective 'lofty/great' modifying کار. Word order (beneficiary — object — verb) is idiomatic.
segments.s540-g25: به خایه نمک بر پراگند زود is an action clause with an omitted subject (they). به + noun marks the target (here 'towards/on the testicles'), نمک is the thing dispersed, and پراگند is an older past/participle form meaning 'scattered' (used like 'scattered [it]'). زود is an adverb ('quickly/soon') modifying the action.
segments.s540-g26: به حقه در آگند بر سان دود describes putting the object into a container. به + حقه expresses direction/target ('into/to the pouch'), در آگند uses آگند (filled) to say the interior became full, and بر سان دود is a comparative phrase ('بر سان' = 'like/as') with دود ('smoke') giving the simile.
segments.s540-g27: هم‌اندر زمان functions adverbially as 'at that same time' or 'meanwhile.' حقه را is the definite object (را marks it), and مهر کرد is a compound verb meaning 'made a seal' or 'sealed' (مهر = seal/stamp; کرد = did/made) in the past tense; the subject is omitted and understood from context.
segments.s540-g28: بیامد is past 'he came.' خروشان is an adverbial/participial modifier meaning 'roaring/turbulent' (describing his manner of coming). The coordination و links the following noun phrase رخساره زرد = 'face/complexion pale' (رخساره = face; زرد = pale/wan).
segments.s540-g29: چو آمد is a temporal clause ('when he came'). به نزدیک is a prepositional phrase marking approach ('to/near'), and تخت بلند is the noun phrase 'the high/throne' (تخت = throne; بلند = high). Word order is typical older Persian: conjunction + verb + prepositional phrase + object.
segments.s540-g30: همان qualifies حقه ('that same pouch'). بنهاد is an archaic past form (بـنهاد) meaning 'placed/laid down' or 'caused to be placed.' با introduces the instruments/ways (مهر و بند = 'with seal and bond'), so the phrase says he put it down sealed and tied.
segments.s540-g31: چنین گفت = 'thus he said' (چنین = thus/so; گفت here introduces direct speech or an admonition). با شاه means 'to the king' (addressing him). کین زینهار is a short admonition—کین 'vengeance' followed by زینهار 'beware/take heed' (so: 'beware of vengeance').
segments.s540-g32: سپارد is a past-tense verb 'he entrusted/placed' (سپرد/سپارد). به گنجور خود indicates the recipient: 'to the treasury (گنجور) of his own (خود)'—خود is an intensifier meaning 'his own.' شهریار at the end identifies/addresses the addressee ('the king'), so the whole clause states that he entrusted it to the king's own treasure-house.
segments.s540-g9: ز + کشتن forms a prepositional phrase 'from killing' (ز is the poetic 'from'). رهانم is an archaic verb form meaning 'set free / spare (me)' with a first‑person object sense; the following مر is the clitic 'me' used here as an object (a redundant/emphatic object clitic common in older Persian). این ماه را is the object phrase 'this moon' (ماه as a poetic epithet for a beautiful person) marked by the object marker را.
segments.s540-g10: مگر here means 'unless / lest'. زین in the text does not carry its literal sense 'saddle' here (it is used idiomatically as 'by this' or 'thereby' in the clause, a sense not listed in the provided options). پشیمان is 'regretful'; کنم is the causal/use-of-'make' verb 'to cause' (I cause/make), and شاه را is the object 'the king' with را marking him as the one affected (i.e. 'make the king regret').
segments.s540-g11: هرانگه is a temporal conjunction 'whenever / when'. کزو is a contracted combination (ک+ ز + او) meaning 'from her / of her' (literally 'from whom'). بچه گردَد is 'a child becomes / comes into being' (گردد = becomes/happens), and جدا 'apart/alone' here stresses an independent offspring — together: 'whenever from her a child is born/separately'.
segments.s540-g12: به جای is the phrase 'in/for the place; in its stead' (جای here = 'place / instead'). آرم is a first‑person verbal form meaning 'I will place / I shall put' in this context (an optative/intentional use in older Persian). این گفته پادشا = 'this statement before the king' (گفته = the said statement, پادشا = king).
segments.s540-g13: نه کاریست = 'it is not a matter / it is not something'. کز is the relative 'that / which'. دل is 'the heart' and همی بگذرد is present habitual 'will pass (away) / will be forgotten' (همی marks the ongoing/present aspect). Together: 'Not a thing that the heart will pass by.'
segments.s540-g14: خردمند باشم uses the first‑person subjunctive/optative 'may I be / I should be' (باشم = 'be (I)') meaning 'I ought to be wise'. به از ... is the comparative construction 'better than ...' (به از + از), so به از بی‌خرد = 'better than being foolish / better than a fool.'
segments.s540-g15: بیاراست is a past/perfective or narrative-form meaning 'he adorned / prepared'. جایی 'a place'; به ایوان خویش = 'in his iwan/porch' (خویش = 'his own', here written as خوی + ش). The verb precedes its objects in classical Persian narrative order.
segments.s540-g16: که introduces a relative clause 'so that / who / which'. دارد ورا = 'he has/keeps her' (دارد = 'possesses/keeps', ورا = 'her / to her'). چون = 'like / as', تن و جان خویش = 'his body and soul (own)' — i.e. he treats/holds her as if she were his body and soul. The clause explains the purpose/quality of the place prepared above.
segments.s540-g17: به زن گفت is a topical prepositional object + verb: literally 'to the woman he said' = 'he said to the woman'. اگر begins a conditional clause. هیچ باد هوا is an idiomatic noun phrase ('هیچ' + 'بادِ هوا' = 'any breeze/whim'); باد and هوا together mean a caprice or sudden fancy.
segments.s540-g18: ببیند is a third-person present/subjunctive used after اگر for the conditional ('if ... sees/is seized by'). ورا is the unstressed oblique pronoun 'to/at her'. من ندارم روا is a compact clause: من (I) + ندارم (I do not have/have not) + روا (permissible/fitting) — idiomatically 'I do not consider it permissible' or 'I will not allow it.'
segments.s540-g19: پس is a temporal/concluding adverb 'then'. اندیشه کرد is the verb phrase 'he thought/pondered' (اندیشه = thought, کرد = did/made = 'did thought'). آنک is an archaic complementizer 'that' introducing the content of his thought; دشمن بسیست uses بسی (many) + -ست (copula) to mean 'enemies are many' (there are many enemies).
segments.s540-g20: گمان بد و نیک is a coordinated noun phrase 'bad and good thoughts/suspicions'. با هرکسیست is an inverted locative/possession-like phrase: با = 'with', هرکسیست is a fused form meaning 'is with every person' (هر کسی + است) — overall: 'bad and good suspicions are with everyone' i.e. 'everyone harbors both good and bad suspicions.'
segments.s540-g21: یکی چاره سازم = 'I will make/devise a certain remedy/plan' (یکی here = 'a certain' / 'one'). سازم is first-person singular present/future 'I make/devise'. که begins a purpose/result clause 'so that'. بدگوی من is a genitive noun phrase 'one who speaks ill of me' (my slanderer).
segments.s540-g22: نراند is a negative jussive/indicative third-person singular of راندن ('to drive/drive away') here in the subordinate clause introduced by که: '(may) not drive'. به ... در جوی من is a prepositional phrase 'into the foul water in my stream' — به + زشت آب = 'into the vile/dirty water', در جوی من specifies 'in my gutter/stream' (جوی من = my stream).
segments.s540-g23: به خانه شد is a common classical phrasing meaning 'he went/returned home' (شد used as intransitive 'came/became at the house'). و links the next action. خایه ببرید is a terse past action 'he cut (the) scrotum/testicle' (خایه = testicle); ببرید is the verbal form used here for 'cut off'. پست functions adverbially as 'low/down' or 'downward' modifying the cutting/position.
segments.s540-g24: برو here is a short narrative marker meaning roughly 'then he (proceeded to)' (literally 'go'), used idiomatically to sequence actions. داغ و دارو نهاد = 'he applied cautery/branding (داغ) and medicine (دارو)' with نهاد (past of نهادن) 'he put/placed/applied'. و ببست 'and he bound/tied (it)' — ببست is past 'he bound/closed'.

sentences.s540-s17: He wrote the history of that upon the coffer, and made its root and origin manifest.
sentences.s540-s1: The mobed said to himself, 'What ill fortune that such an order has come from the king.'
sentences.s540-s2: We are all of one mind about death — young and old alike; King Ardashir has no son.
sentences.s540-s3: If he were to count countless years, when the throne passes it would fall into the hands of the enemy.
sentences.s540-s4: Therefore — since this affair is unprofitable — I will assign one great deed to a single man.
sentences.s540-s13: They quickly scattered salt on the testicles and put them into the pouch, which became filled inside like smoke.
sentences.s540-s14: At that same time he sealed the pouch; he came in roaring, his face pale.
sentences.s540-s15: When he came near the high throne, he placed that same pouch there, sealed and bound.
sentences.s540-s16: Thus he said to the king, 'Beware of vengeance,' and he entrusted it to the king's own treasury.
sentences.s540-s5: Spare this moon (this beauty) from my killing, lest thereby I make the king repent.
sentences.s540-s6: Whenever she bears a child, I will put this statement before the king in its place.
sentences.s540-s7: It is not something the heart will simply pass over; I should be wise rather than foolish.
sentences.s540-s8: He set up (adorned) a place in his iwan, for he keeps her there as he would his body and soul.
sentences.s540-s9: He said to the woman, 'If any whim/caprice seizes her, I will not allow it.'
sentences.s540-s10: Then he reflected that enemies are many, and that every person has both bad and good suspicions.
sentences.s540-s11: I will devise a certain remedy so that those who slander me do not drive me into the foul water of my stream.
sentences.s540-s12: He went home and cut off the testicle; then he applied cautery and medicine and bound it up.
